questions.question: An experiment is set up as shown. cotton wool soaked in ammonia cotton wool soaked in hydrochloric acid After several minutes, a white ring of ammonium chloride appears as shown. ammonium chloride Which statement explains the observation after several minutes? A Ammonia gas diffuses faster than hydrogen chloride gas because its molecules have a lower molecular mass., B Ammonia  gas  diffuses  faster  than  hydrogen  chloride  gas  because its  molecules  have  a higher molecular mass., C Ammonia gas diffuses slower than hydrogen chloride gas because its molecules have a lower molecular mass., D Ammonia gas diffuses slower than hydrogen chloride gas because its molecules have a higher molecular mass.
questions.text: An experiment is set up as shown. cotton wool soaked in ammonia cotton wool soaked in hydrochloric acid After several minutes, a white ring of ammonium chloride appears as shown. ammonium chloride Which statement explains the observation after several minutes? A Ammonia gas diffuses faster than hydrogen chloride gas because its molecules have a lower molecular mass., B Ammonia  gas  diffuses  faster  than  hydrogen  chloride  gas  because its  molecules  have  a higher molecular mass., C Ammonia gas diffuses slower than hydrogen chloride gas because its molecules have a lower molecular mass., D Ammonia gas diffuses slower than hydrogen chloride gas because its molecules have a higher molecular mass.
options: A Ammonia gas diffuses faster than hydrogen chloride gas because its molecules have a lower molecular mass.
B Ammonia  gas  diffuses  faster  than  hydrogen  chloride  gas  because its  molecules  have  a higher molecular mass.
C Ammonia gas diffuses slower than hydrogen chloride gas because its molecules have a lower molecular mass.
D Ammonia gas diffuses slower than hydrogen chloride gas because its molecules have a higher molecular mass.

questions.question: A student put exactly 25.00 cm 3 of dilute hydrochloric acid into a conical flask. The student added 2.5 g of solid sodium carbonate and measured the change in temperature of the mixture. Which apparatus does the student need to use? A balance, measuring cylinder, thermometer, B balance, pipette, stopwatch, C balance, pipette, thermometer, D burette, pipette, thermometer 3
questions.text: A student put exactly 25.00 cm 3 of dilute hydrochloric acid into a conical flask. The student added 2.5 g of solid sodium carbonate and measured the change in temperature of the mixture. Which apparatus does the student need to use? A balance, measuring cylinder, thermometer, B balance, pipette, stopwatch, C balance, pipette, thermometer, D burette, pipette, thermometer 3

questions.question: A student separates sugar from pieces of broken glass by dissolving the sugar in water and filtering off the broken glass. filtrate What is the filtrate? A broken glass only, B broken glass and sugar solution, C pure water, D sugar solution
questions.text: A student separates sugar from pieces of broken glass by dissolving the sugar in water and filtering off the broken glass. filtrate What is the filtrate? A broken glass only, B broken glass and sugar solution, C pure water, D sugar solution
options: B broken glass and sugar solution

questions.question: Which statement describes the attractive forces between molecules? A They are strong covalent bonds which hold molecules together., B They are strong ionic bonds which hold molecules together., C They are weak forces formed between covalently-bonded molecules., D They are weak forces which hold ions together in a lattice.
questions.text: Which statement describes the attractive forces between molecules? A They are strong covalent bonds which hold molecules together., B They are strong ionic bonds which hold molecules together., C They are weak forces formed between covalently-bonded molecules., D They are weak forces which hold ions together in a lattice.
options: A They are strong covalent bonds which hold molecules together.
B They are strong ionic bonds which hold molecules together.
C They are weak forces formed between covalently-bonded molecules.
D They are weak forces which hold ions together in a lattice.

questions.question: Which statements about hydrogen are correct? 1   When hydrogen is burned, heat energy is released. 2   When hydrogen is used in a fuel cell, electrical energy is generated. 3   When hydrogen is used as a fuel, water is the only product. A 1, 2 and 3 B  1 and 2 only C 1 only D 3 only
questions.text: Which statements about hydrogen are correct? 1   When hydrogen is burned, heat energy is released. 2   When hydrogen is used in a fuel cell, electrical energy is generated. 3   When hydrogen is used as a fuel, water is the only product. A 1, 2 and 3 B  1 and 2 only C 1 only D 3 only
options: A 1, 2 and 3 B  1 and 2 only C 1 only D 3 only

questions.question: Iron( II) chloride solution reacts with chlorine gas. The equation is shown. 2FeCl 2 (aq)  +  Cl 2 (g)    2FeCl 3 (aq) Which statements about this reaction are correct? 1   Fe 2+ ions are reduced to Fe 3+ ions. 2   Chlorine acts as a reducing agent. 3   Fe 2+ ions each lose an electron. 4   Cl 2 molecules are reduced to Cl – ions. A 1 and 2 B  2 and 3 C  2 and 4 D  3 and 4
questions.text: Iron( II) chloride solution reacts with chlorine gas. The equation is shown. 2FeCl 2 (aq)  +  Cl 2 (g)    2FeCl 3 (aq) Which statements about this reaction are correct? 1   Fe 2+ ions are reduced to Fe 3+ ions. 2   Chlorine acts as a reducing agent. 3   Fe 2+ ions each lose an electron. 4   Cl 2 molecules are reduced to Cl – ions. A 1 and 2 B  2 and 3 C  2 and 4 D  3 and 4
options: A 1 and 2 B  2 and 3 C  2 and 4 D  3 and 4

questions.question: Which statements about acids and bases are correct? 1   An acid reacts with a metal to give off hydrogen. 2   A base reacts with an ammonium salt to give off ammonia. 3   An acid reacts with a carbonate to give off carbon dioxide. 4   Alkaline solutions are orange in methyl orange. A 1, 2 and 3 B  1, 2 and 4 C  1, 3 and 4 D  2, 3 and 4 7

options: A addition of bromine to propene
B esterification of ethanol and ethanoic acid
D substitution of methane with chlorine

A Element R does not conduct electricity., B Elements R and Y react together to form an ionic compound., C Element Z exists as a diatomic molecule., D Element Z reacts with element T.
questions.text: A period of the Periodic Table is shown. group    I   II   III   IV   V   VI   VII  VIII element   R   S   T   V   W   X   Y   Z The letters are not their chemical symbols. Which statement is correct? A Element R does not conduct electricity., B Elements R and Y react together to form an ionic compound., C Element Z exists as a diatomic molecule., D Element Z reacts with element T.
options: A Element R does not conduct electricity.
B Elements R and Y react together to form an ionic compound.
C Element Z exists as a diatomic molecule.
D Element Z reacts with element T.

questions.question: All metal nitrates are soluble in water. All metal chlorides are soluble except silver and lead. All metal carbonates are insoluble except sodium and potassium. Which aqueous solutions produce a precipitate when mixed together? 1   silver nitrate + sodium carbonate 2   silver nitrate + sodium chloride 3   barium nitrate + potassium chloride A 1 and 2 only B  1 and 3 only C  2 and 3 only D  1, 2 and 3
questions.text: All metal nitrates are soluble in water. All metal chlorides are soluble except silver and lead. All metal carbonates are insoluble except sodium and potassium. Which aqueous solutions produce a precipitate when mixed together? 1   silver nitrate + sodium carbonate 2   silver nitrate + sodium chloride 3   barium nitrate + potassium chloride A 1 and 2 only B  1 and 3 only C  2 and 3 only D  1, 2 and 3
options: A 1 and 2 only B  1 and 3 only C  2 and 3 only D  1, 2 and 3

questions.question: Which row describes properties of transition elements? property 1 property 2 property 3 A coloured compounds high density variable oxidation states, B high density high melting point one oxidation state, C high melting point coloured compounds one oxidation state, D low melting point high density variable oxidation states 9
questions.text: Which row describes properties of transition elements? property 1 property 2 property 3 A coloured compounds high density variable oxidation states, B high density high melting point one oxidation state, C high melting point coloured compounds one oxidation state, D low melting point high density variable oxidation states 9

B high density high melting point one oxidation state
C high melting point coloured compounds one oxidation state
D low melting point high density variable oxidation states 9

questions.question: The noble gases are in Group VIII of the Periodic Table. Which statement explains why noble gases are unreactive? A They all have eight electrons in their outer shells., B They all have full outer shells., C They are all gases., D They are all monoatomic.
questions.text: The noble gases are in Group VIII of the Periodic Table. Which statement explains why noble gases are unreactive? A They all have eight electrons in their outer shells., B They all have full outer shells., C They are all gases., D They are all monoatomic.
options: A They all have eight electrons in their outer shells.
B They all have full outer shells.

questions.question: Which statement is correct for all metals? A They conduct electricity when molten., B They gain electrons when they form ions., C They have a low density., D They have a low melting point.
questions.text: Which statement is correct for all metals? A They conduct electricity when molten., B They gain electrons when they form ions., C They have a low density., D They have a low melting point.
options: A They conduct electricity when molten.
B They gain electrons when they form ions.
C They have a low density.
D They have a low melting point.

questions.question: Carbon dioxide is produced during the extraction of aluminium from bauxite. Which statement describes how this carbon dioxide is made? A Carbon monoxide reduces aluminium oxide forming carbon dioxide and aluminium., B Carbon is burned in the blast furnace to release heat energy., C Oxygen made in the process reacts with the carbon electrode., D The ore of aluminium undergoes thermal decomposition.
questions.text: Carbon dioxide is produced during the extraction of aluminium from bauxite. Which statement describes how this carbon dioxide is made? A Carbon monoxide reduces aluminium oxide forming carbon dioxide and aluminium., B Carbon is burned in the blast furnace to release heat energy., C Oxygen made in the process reacts with the carbon electrode., D The ore of aluminium undergoes thermal decomposition.
options: A Carbon monoxide reduces aluminium oxide forming carbon dioxide and aluminium.
B Carbon is burned in the blast furnace to release heat energy.
C Oxygen made in the process reacts with the carbon electrode.
D The ore of aluminium undergoes thermal decomposition.

questions.question: Aluminium objects do not need protection from corrosion. Iron objects must be protected from corrosion. Which statement explains why aluminium resists corrosion? A Aluminium does not form ions easily., B Aluminium does not react with water or air., C Aluminium has a protective oxide layer., D Aluminium is below iron in the reactivity series. 10

B Aluminium does not react with water or air.
C Aluminium has a protective oxide layer.
D Aluminium is below iron in the reactivity series. 10

questions.question: Which statements explain why zinc is used to protect iron from rusting? 1   Zinc is more reactive than iron. 2   Zinc is less reactive than iron. 3   Zinc can form alloys with iron. 4   Zinc acts as a sacrificial metal. A 1 and 3 B 1 and 4 C 2 and 3 D 2 and 4
questions.text: Which statements explain why zinc is used to protect iron from rusting? 1   Zinc is more reactive than iron. 2   Zinc is less reactive than iron. 3   Zinc can form alloys with iron. 4   Zinc acts as a sacrificial metal. A 1 and 3 B 1 and 4 C 2 and 3 D 2 and 4
options: A 1 and 3 B 1 and 4 C 2 and 3 D 2 and 4
